title: Hawke's Bay Family Budget
meta_desc: Discover how to visit Hawke's Bay with your family on a budget of $180-340/day, including free activities, accommodation options and top attractions
intro: Hawke's Bay is a hidden family gem, with Art Deco Napier, Cape Kidnappers gannets and NZ's best fruit. For a family of four, daily costs can range from $180 to $340. With some planning, you can have a fantastic holiday without breaking the bank.

sections.heading: Realistic Daily Costs
sections.body:
A family of four can expect to pay between $180 and $340 per day in Hawke's Bay, depending on accommodation choices and activities. This estimate includes food, transport and attractions.

For example, a meal at a mid-range restaurant can cost around $80 for a family of four, while a self-catering apartment can cost around $150 per night.

sections.heading: Free Activities
sections.body:
Hawke's Bay has plenty of free activities to enjoy, including the Napier Art Deco walk, Marine Parade and Waimarama Beach.

These activities are not only free but also offer a great way to experience the region's unique culture and natural beauty.

sections.heading: Accommodation Options
sections.body:
Hawke's Bay has a range of accommodation options to suit all budgets, from camping and backpackers to mid-range hotels and self-catering apartments.

For a family of four, a self-catering apartment or holiday home can be a cost-effective option, with prices starting from around $150 per night.

sections.heading: Budget Breakdown
sections.body: Here's a rough 3-day budget breakdown for a family of four in Hawke's Bay:

sections.heading: Saving Money and Splurging
sections.body:
To save money in Hawke's Bay, consider self-catering, preparing your own meals and taking advantage of free activities.

However, some experiences are worth splurging on, such as a guided tour of the Cape Kidnappers gannet colony or a visit to the Napier Aquarium.
